perturbation.original_text: Upon the request of Casino, Receiving Party shall immediately return all Confidential Information received in written or tangible form, including all notes, copies, o r media containing such Confidential Information. Alternatively, Casino may direct R eceiving Party to destroy such Confidential Information, in which case Receiving Party shall confirm such destruction in writing within twenty (20) days thereafter.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon the request of Casino, Receiving Party shall return all Confidential Information received in written or tangible form, including all notes, copies, o r media containing such Confidential Information within ninety (90) days. Alternatively, the Casino may request, but is not required to direct, the Receiving Party to destroy such Confidential Information. The Receiving Party is not required to confirm destruction in writing.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text creates an in-text contradiction by changing the timeframe for returning Confidential Information from 'immediately' to 'within ninety (90) days' and removing the requirement to confirm destruction of Confidential Information in writing. The original intent of immediate return is now delayed, and proof of destruction is no longer required, which conflicts with other sections of the contract emphasizing confidentiality. This introduces uncertainty regarding the enforcement of immediate retrieval of sensitive information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Upon the request of Casino, Receiving Party shall return all Confidential Information received in written or tangible form, including all notes, copies, o r media containing such Confidential Information within ninety (90) days. Alternatively, the Casino may request, but is not required to direct, the Receiving Party to destroy such Confidential Information. The Receiving Party is not required to confirm destruction in writing.
perturbation.justification: The changed text contradicts the original text by altering the timeframe for returning Confidential Information from 'immediately' to 'within ninety (90) days' and removing the requirement to confirm destruction of Confidential Information in writing, as stated in the Perturbation Explanation. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text and thus still retains the contradiction. This aligns with the 'Inconsistencies' definition where different sections of a contract provide conflicting deadlines, obligations, or penalties, creating an in-text contradiction regarding which terms should be enforced.

perturbation.original_text: Receiving Party shall not disclose any Confidential Information to any other person or entity other than as provided herein, without Casino’s prior written cons ent. Neither party nor any of its representatives shall contact, either directly or indirectly, any governmental official, lender or other third party (other than a repre sentative as provided herein) to discuss the Project or the business or the assets of the other party, or a potential business transaction with or c oncerning the other party, without first obtaining the written consent of the other party.
perturbation.changed_text: Receiving Party may disclose Confidential Information to other persons or entities with Casino's implied consent. Either party or their representatives may contact a third-party lender to discuss potential funding for the project.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by stating that the Receiving Party may disclose Confidential Information to others with 'implied consent,' contradicting the original requirement for 'prior written consent.' Additionally, it allows either party to contact a third-party lender to discuss potential funding, removing the initial restriction of prior written consent for contacting external parties. This creates uncertainty in enforcing the confidentiality terms.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Receiving Party may disclose Confidential Information to other persons or entities with Casino's implied consent. Either party or their representatives may contact a third-party lender to discuss potential funding for the project.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original text and creates an in-text contradiction as it allows for 'implied consent' for disclosing Confidential Information, which conflicts with the original requirement of 'prior written consent.' The changed text and contradicted text are identical, and thus creates a contradiction. The original text also prevents contact to third parties unless with written concent but the changed text allows it

perturbation.original_text: T his A greement shall ha ve a term of three (3) years. Receiving P arty’s obligations in relation to technical and personally- identifiable inf ormation shall be inde finite.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall have a term of three (3) years. Receiving Party’s obligations in relation to all Confidential Information shall expire after one (1) year.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text creates an in-text contradiction by stating that the Receiving Party's obligations for *all* Confidential Information expire after one year, which directly contradicts the original clause specifying that obligations related to technical and personally-identifiable information are indefinite. This introduces uncertainty about the duration of confidentiality obligations, especially concerning sensitive data.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall have a term of three (3) years. Receiving Party’s obligations in relation to all Confidential Information shall expire after one (1) year.
perturbation.justification: The modified text states that all confidentiality obligations expire after one year, while the original text specified that obligations related to technical and personally-identifiable information are indefinite. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text which shows that the change directly contradicts the original agreement regarding the duration of confidentiality for sensitive data, creating a clear in-text contradiction as outlined in the Perturbation Explanation and aligning with the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition.
